name: Axolotl
pricing_notes: Fully open source (Apache 2.0); no API costs; GPU compute is the cost

auth.notes: YAML config-driven; HuggingFace token for Hub integration

performance.notes: Training performance competitive with TRL+PEFT; Flash Attention 2 integration for speed

agent_readiness.notes: Configuration-driven LLM fine-tuning framework. YAML config for all training hyperparameters — no code changes needed. Supports LoRA, QLoRA, full fine-tuning, DPO, RLHF. Wide model support (Llama, Mistral, Gemma, Qwen, Phi). Flash Attention 2. Multi-GPU via DeepSpeed/FSDP. Cloud notebook + modal.com integration. Popular in open-source fine-tuning community.

gotchas: YAML configuration has steep learning curve with many options
Debugging training issues harder than TRL (less programmatic control)
Breaking changes between versions can require config updates
Documentation quality varies — community resources often needed
best_when: Practitioners wanting configuration-driven fine-tuning without writing training code
Rapid experimentation across multiple base models and configurations
Community fine-tuning of popular OSS models (Llama, Mistral, Gemma)
Teams without deep PyTorch expertise wanting reliable fine-tuning
avoid_when: Teams needing custom training loops or unconventional architectures
Production ML engineering requiring tight code control (use TRL directly)
Novel research requiring custom trainers not in YAML config
summary: Axolotl is a configuration-driven LLM fine-tuning framework where all training parameters are specified via YAML config — no training code required. Supports LoRA, QLoRA, DPO, RLHF across Llama, Mistral, Gemma, Qwen, Phi models. Flash Attention 2 and multi-GPU via DeepSpeed/FSDP. Popular in open-source community for its ease of use. Best for practitioners wanting reproducible fine-tuning runs without custom code.
